ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for salted egg noodle; 'noodle dish' (FOODON_03315595) is the closest defensible parent class. Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original is a Singapore-branded instant/prepared noodle product with salted egg yolk seasoning.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta and noodles, cooked) is the closest defensible category. No specific code exists for salted egg flavoured noodle products in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific branded noodle product. SNOMED CT does not maintain codes for commercial food brand variants.

preparation_method: mixed with salted egg yolk sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original is approximately 100g, equivalent to one individual packet of the prepared noodle dish.

nutrient_source.title: Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate are taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original (cr_id G00010645). The HPB record reports fat as 0.0g/100g; this is documented in notes as a confirmed zero per the HPB source, though it is acknowledged as likely reflecting incomplete data for the full product. Sodium is estimated from published literature on salted egg yolk products and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable salted egg noodle products, as HPB does not report sodium for this entry. Secondary nutrients (saturated fat, sugar, dietary fibre) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for comparable instant noodle products with salted egg seasoning.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original. GI of 65 is estimated by analogy with cooked wheat noodles (GI ~53–65, medium category) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The HPB-reported carbohydrate of 2.36g/100g is anomalously low and likely reflects incomplete data (seasoning only); the GI estimate is based on the noodle component as the primary carbohydrate vehicle. Glycemic load per 100g serving = (65 × 2.36) / 100 = 1.53, rounded to 1.5, which is very low due to the low reported carbohydrate. If the full product carbohydrate is ~40–60g/100g, GL would be substantially higher (~26–39 per 100g). Users should verify complete product nutrition data.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high estimated sodium (~820mg/100g) from salted egg yolk seasoning and flavour enhancers, exceeding the 600mg/100g threshold for hypertension concern.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original due to its very high sodium content (~820mg/100g from salted egg yolk and seasoning), which can significantly elevate blood pressure; even a single 100g serving delivers more than half the recommended daily sodium limit of 1500mg for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: HPB-reported carbohydrate of 2.36g/100g is anomalously low and likely reflects incomplete data; the full noodle product likely contains 40–60g carbohydrate/100g, which would significantly impact postprandial blood glucose in type 2 diabetes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should treat the reported carbohydrate value (2.36g/100g) with caution as it likely reflects seasoning data only; the complete noodle dish is expected to have a medium glycemic index (~65) and substantially higher carbohydrate load, requiring careful portion control and blood glucose monitoring after consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium from salted egg yolk seasoning and ultra-processed formulation with flavour enhancers; salted egg yolk is also a source of dietary cholesterol and saturated fat, both of which are relevant to cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original due to its high estimated sodium (~820mg/100g), ultra-processed nature, and the presence of salted egg yolk which contributes dietary cholesterol and saturated fat, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium from salted egg yolk worsens fluid retention and joint swelling; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original as its high sodium content (~820mg/100g) exacerbates joint swelling and tissue inflammation, and the ultra-processed formulation provides no omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or other anti-inflammatory factors.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Original is a Singapore-branded ultra-processed noodle dish featuring wheat noodles coated in a rich, creamy salted egg yolk sauce. The product is produced by Irvin's, a well-known Singapore food brand celebrated for its salted egg yolk snack range. The original variant delivers the signature savoury-umami flavour of salted duck egg yolk combined with butter and curry leaves, without the added heat of chilli found in spicy variants. The texture is soft and saucy, with the noodles absorbing the intensely flavoured salted egg coating. Nutritionally, the HPB Foodo database reports energy of 55 kcal/100g, protein 10.7g/100g, fat 0.0g/100g, and carbohydrate 2.36g/100g; however, these values are widely considered to reflect incomplete data, likely capturing only the seasoning component rather than the full noodle product. The full dish is expected to contain approximately 40–60g carbohydrate/100g and significantly higher fat from the salted egg yolk and butter base. Sodium is estimated at approximately 820mg/100g based on the salted egg yolk seasoning profile, making this product a high-sodium food. Clinically, this product is unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its very high sodium and saturated fat content from salted egg yolk. For type 2 diabetes, the reported carbohydrate is misleadingly low; the actual glycemic impact of the full product is expected to be moderate to high. The product is not halal-certified in all markets and contains gluten, egg, milk, and soy allergens. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner dish in Singapore and is popular as a comfort food and social sharing dish.

confidence_reason: The HPB Foodo database is an approved primary source for Singapore foods and provides an exact match for this product (cr_id G00010645). However, the reported macronutrient values (particularly fat 0.0g and carbohydrate 2.36g) are anomalously low and are widely understood to reflect incomplete data, likely capturing only the seasoning component. Sodium is estimated rather than directly sourced. Secondary nutrients are estimated from analogous USDA FoodData Central entries. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): fat_g is reported as 0.0g/100g per the HPB Foodo database entry for this product. This is a confirmed zero per the HPB source; however, it is acknowledged as likely reflecting incomplete data for the full noodle product (seasoning-only data). The full Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle product is expected to contain meaningful fat from salted egg yolk and butter. saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 consistent with the HPB fat report; this is confirmed zero per HPB source but similarly may reflect incomplete data. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all set to -1: detailed micronutrient, fatty acid profile, and moisture data for this specific Irvin's branded noodle product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. DATA QUALITY CAVEAT: The HPB-reported macronutrient values for this product are anomalously low (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7g, fat 0.0g, carbohydrate 2.36g per 100g) and are believed to reflect incomplete data, possibly capturing only the salted egg yolk seasoning component rather than the full noodle dish. Users and clinicians should verify complete product nutritional information from the product packaging before making dietary decisions. SODIUM ESTIMATION: Sodium of 820mg/100g is estimated based on published data for salted egg yolk products and comparable branded salted egg noodle products; this is not directly reported by HPB for this entry. HALAL STATUS: Irvin's Salted Egg products are not universally halal-certified; consumers should verify current certification status. GI NOTE: GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on cooked wheat noodles from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. The glycemic load of 1.5 per 100g serving is based on the anomalously low reported carbohydrate and would be substantially higher if the full product carbohydrate (~40–60g/100g) is used.
